frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current percepts are attracted toward recently seen stimuli

frameworks.name: feature binding
frameworks.one_line: object representation as a conjunction of multiple features

core_question: Does serial dependence operate at the level of individual features, integrated objects, or both?
key_assumptions: perceptual reports reflect attraction toward recent stimuli
objects can be decomposed into bound features (shape, emotional expression)
manipulations: reported feature: shape vs emotional expression
feature composition of the object across trials
key_findings: reports of both shape and emotional expression were attracted toward recently seen stimuli
serial dependence arose at the feature level and at the integrated-object level
shape serial dependence was largely independent of other co-defining features
emotional-expression serial dependence operated on the object-level conjunction
interpretations: the level at which serial dependence operates depends on the feature/object type
perceptual history bias can attach to features or to bound object representations
